postmanEvidence: Postman Desktop 11.71.7 packages postman-runtime 7.50.0, postman-collection 5.2.0, and @postman/tough-cookie 4.1.3-postman.1.
The Newman 6.2.2 probe records script readability and mutation behavior for HttpOnly cookies through pm.cookies, pm.cookies.jar(), and pm.sendRequest response-cookie updates.

desktopSourceEvidence: The installed Desktop runtime bundle includes a CookieStore serializer/deserializer that round-trips httpOnly on persisted cookies.

The installed Desktop runtime uses @postman/tough-cookie CookieJar/CookieStore for request cookie storage and mutation; the storage layer does not strip httpOnly values while serializing or deserializing cookies.

mutations.jar.set(baseUrl, { name, value, httpOnly: creates a readable HttpOnly cookie that is sent on later requests
mutations.jar.set(baseUrl, 'replaceMe', 'client-vi: replaces an existing HttpOnly cookie with the visible value
mutations.jar.clear(baseUrl): removes visible and HttpOnly cookies in scope

pmSendRequest.responseSetCookie: pm.sendRequest response Set-Cookie with HttpOnly updates the shared jar and is readable through jar.get before the next request

changes: PostMeter no longer hides HttpOnly values from default Postman-compatible script cookie helpers.
Current-request pm.cookies.get/has/toObject are synchronous for Postman compatibility while retaining callback compatibility for existing PostMeter scripts.
pm.cookies.jar().getAll returns a Postman CookieList facade instead of a plain object.
Brokered jar unset and clear operations can remove HttpOnly cookies when script cookie access is enabled.
pm.sendRequest response-cookie jar updates preserve and expose HttpOnly values through the brokered cookie helpers.
securityBoundary: Scripts still cannot access browser, Electron, Node, filesystem, shell, process, or raw network primitives.
HttpOnly access remains gated by the existing workspace script-cookie capability; disabling script cookie access blocks pm.cookies and pm.cookies.jar() entirely.

limitations: This artifact combines installed Desktop runtime/source inspection with a Newman behavioral probe. It is not an automated GUI run of Postman Desktop.

Postman/Newman do not expose pm.cookies.unset in the observed current-request CookieList shape, so current-request removal remains a PostMeter compatibility extension and is not used as evidence for the Postman behavior row.
